description: A CLI tool that diagnoses weak or broken pytest suites and provides a 0-100 health score with actionable recommendations

tasks.title: Initialize Python project with uv package manager
tasks.description: Set up pyproject.toml with uv, define dependencies (pytest, ruff, vulture, click, etc.)
acceptance_criteria: Project has uv.lock and pyproject.toml configured
uv can install and run the project
All core dependencies are specified

tasks.title: Create CLI entry point
tasks.description: Build the main CLI interface using Click framework with basic argument parsing
acceptance_criteria: CLI accepts directory path as input
CLI supports --verbose, --fix, --diff, --json, --output flags
pytest-doctor . command runs without errors

tasks.title: Implement configuration system
tasks.description: Support configuration from pytest-doctor.config.json, pyproject.toml, and CLI flags with proper precedence
acceptance_criteria: Config loaded from pytest-doctor.config.json when present
Config loaded from pyproject.toml [tool.pytest-doctor] section
CLI flags override config file settings
Default configuration is applied when no config present

tasks.title: Implement Ruff linting integration
tasks.description: Integrate ruff for correctness, performance, security, and async issue detection in test files
acceptance_criteria: Ruff runs on test files and captures violations
Results include file paths, line numbers, rule names, and messages
Can filter results based on ignore configuration
Respects --diff flag to check only changed files

tasks.title: Implement Vulture dead code detection
tasks.description: Integrate vulture to find unused test utilities, fixtures, and helper functions
acceptance_criteria: Vulture runs on test files and identifies dead code
Results include file paths, line numbers, confidence scores, and descriptions
Can filter results based on ignore configuration
Distinguishes between truly unused code and false positives

tasks.title: Implement test quality analysis engine
tasks.description: Build custom analysis to detect fixture isolation issues, missing parametrization, slow tests, and coverage gaps
acceptance_criteria: Detects fixture isolation issues (shared state, side effects)
Identifies tests missing parametrization for multiple cases
Flags slow/performance-problematic tests
Highlights missing test coverage for logical paths
Results include severity levels and line numbers

tasks.title: Implement gap analysis for coverage
tasks.description: Detect logic paths, edge cases, and risk areas that tests miss
acceptance_criteria: Identifies untested code paths in the project
Flags edge cases that should be tested
Highlights high-risk areas with insufficient test coverage
Provides specific locations and recommendations

tasks.title: Implement health score calculation
tasks.description: Create a 0-100 scoring system based on aggregated analysis results with thresholds (Critical <50, Needs work 50-74, Good 75+)
acceptance_criteria: Score ranges from 0-100
Score considers linting violations, dead code, test quality, and coverage
Thresholds: Critical <50, Needs work 50-74, Good 75+
Weight distribution is configurable
Score calculation is deterministic and reproducible

tasks.title: Implement results aggregation
tasks.description: Combine results from all three analysis engines with deduplication and prioritization
acceptance_criteria: Results are combined from all three analysis engines
Duplicate issues are removed
Results are prioritized by severity
Each result includes source (linting/dead-code/quality)
Results are grouped by file for better presentation

tasks.title: Implement actionable recommendations system
tasks.description: Generate structured, agent-friendly recommendations for each detected issue
acceptance_criteria: Each issue has a clear, actionable recommendation
Recommendations are formatted for easy agent consumption
Recommendations include suggested fixes or improvements
Priority levels guide agent fix ordering

tasks.title: Implement human-readable console output
tasks.description: Create formatted terminal output with health score, summary, and detailed findings
acceptance_criteria: Displays overall health score prominently
Shows summary of findings by category
Includes detailed issue list with file paths and line numbers
Color-coded severity levels (Critical/Warning/Info)
Respects --verbose flag for additional detail

tasks.title: Implement JSON output format
tasks.description: Create structured JSON output for agent integration via --json flag and --output parameter
acceptance_criteria: --json flag outputs complete diagnostics in JSON
--output flag writes JSON to specified file
JSON includes all issues, severity, file paths, line numbers, recommendations
JSON schema is documented and stable
JSON output is parseable by external agents

tasks.title: Implement --diff flag for changed files
tasks.description: Support scanning only modified files compared to a git ref (e.g., main branch)
acceptance_criteria: --diff main flag scans only changed files
Results show only issues in modified lines
Git integration works reliably
Performance improved for incremental checks

tasks.title: Implement --fix flag for agent integration
tasks.description: Support automated fixes via remote coding agents with deeplinks and structured output
acceptance_criteria: --fix flag generates agent-friendly output
Deeplinks include project context and diagnostics
Format compatible with remote coding agents
User can apply fixes through agent interface

tasks.title: Implement parallel analysis execution
tasks.description: Run linting, dead code detection, and test quality analysis in parallel for performance
acceptance_criteria: All three analysis engines run concurrently
Thread/process safety is maintained
Performance improvement is measurable
No race conditions or data corruption

tasks.title: Write comprehensive test suite
tasks.description: Create unit and integration tests for all major components
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests for scoring system
Unit tests for analysis engines
Integration tests for CLI
Test coverage >80%
All tests pass

tasks.title: Create usage documentation
tasks.description: Document CLI usage, configuration, and integration with agents
acceptance_criteria: README has installation instructions
Configuration options documented
CLI flags documented with examples
Agent integration examples provided
JSON schema documented

notes: All three analysis engines (linting, dead code, test quality) should be parallelizable
Configuration system should support both file-based and CLI-flag overrides
Agent integration should be flexible to support both local and remote agents
JSON output format should be stable and well-documented for agent compatibility
